title: Review Compression System
consumer_purpose: Produce a weighted review manifest per campaign showing developers only what they actually need to review, not every changed line
claim_atoms: Create rig.relay.review_manifest.v1 schema — the output contract for the review compression system with sections: plan_summary, diff_summary, plan_vs_diff_deviation, validation_summary, red_team_summary, review_weight_map, residual_risks, provenance
Create rig.relay.builder_publication_record.v1 schema — canonical schema aligning with existing publication_record.v2 example
Create rig.relay.verification_record.v1 schema — canonical schema for reviewer verification records
Create rig.relay.prepublication_review_cycle.v1 schema — standalone schema for review cycles (currently embedded inline)
Design comparison engine architecture: how to match plan claim_atoms/boundary to actual Snapshot.FileDiff[] producing deviation reports

Design review weight rubric: mechanical rename=low, generated file=hidden, security boundary=high, IPC bridge=high, test-only=medium, diff hunk not in plan=high, code changed after validation=critical
Design integration with existing Snapshot.Service: consume diffFull() output as primary diff source
Produce implementation plan with phased approach — schema first, then comparison engine, then weight rubric, then manifest generator tool, then CLI output
revision_reason: Incorporating critic findings: convergence requires plan `files` field, validation needs normalizer module, error handling needs degradation tiers, weight rubric needs audit trail, and scope must be split into 3 tracks (prerequisites, core manifest, weight rubric enhancement). Impact assessor also revealed: builder_publication_record must reconcile v1/v2 via oneOf, prepublication_review_cycle schema must align with 3 existing tools, BinderService is stubbed (engine must not depend on it), and cross-lane seam with IN-003 requires coordination.
